name: Silver Arm of Nuada
description: The Silver Arm of Nuada, also known as Airgetlám, is a significant artifact in Irish mythology, symbolizing kingship, leadership, and the resilience of the Tuatha Dé Danann. Nuada, the first king of the Tuatha Dé Danann, lost his arm in the First Battle of Mag Tuired against the Fir Bolg. Because a king was required to be physically perfect, Nuada was forced to abdicate. The physician Dian Cécht and the wright Creidhne crafted a fully functional arm of silver for Nuada, restoring him to his former glory and enabling him to reclaim his throne. The arm wasn't merely a replacement; it was imbued with magical properties, allowing Nuada to wield weapons and lead his people with renewed strength. However, the arm's perfection was later surpassed by Miach, Dian Cécht's son, who replaced it with an arm of flesh and blood grown from Nuada's severed limb. This act of surpassing his father's creation led to Miach's tragic death. The Silver Arm represents the ingenuity and craftsmanship of the Tuatha Dé Danann, their commitment to their king, and the importance of physical perfection in leadership. It is a testament to their ability to overcome adversity and their dedication to maintaining their sovereignty. The arm also carries a cautionary tale about ambition and the consequences of challenging established authority. The legacy of the Silver Arm continues to resonate in Irish folklore, reminding us of the power of innovation, the burdens of leadership, and the cyclical nature of power and ambition. Its creation and subsequent replacement highlight the tension between artificial perfection and the natural state, a recurring theme in many mythologies. The Silver Arm is more than just a prosthetic; it is a potent symbol of kingship, resilience, and the enduring spirit of the Tuatha Dé Danann. Its story is a cornerstone of Irish mythology, illustrating the complex relationships between gods, mortals, and the objects that define their destinies.
subtitle: Symbol of kingship and resilience.
origin: Crafted by Dian Cécht and Creidhne to replace Nuada's lost arm.
currentLocation: Lost to history, likely in the Otherworld.

keyMyths.title: The First Battle of Mag Tuired
keyMyths.description: Nuada loses his arm in battle against the Fir Bolg, rendering him unfit to rule. This necessitates the creation of the Silver Arm.

keyMyths.title: The Crafting of the Silver Arm
keyMyths.description: Dian Cécht and Creidhne create the Silver Arm, restoring Nuada to his former status as king. The arm is a symbol of their skill and dedication.

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
sources.description: A compilation of early Irish myths and sagas, including accounts of Nuada and the Silver Arm.
sources.text: A compilation of early Irish myths and sagas, including accounts of Nuada and the Silver Arm.
